title: An Abundance of Katherines
author: John Green
summary: A washed-up child prodigy who only dates girls named Katherine sets off on a road trip after his latest breakup, hoping a mathematical theorem can predict love.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
summary: A young Andalusian shepherd travels from Spain to the Egyptian desert in search of treasure, discovering along the way what it means to follow one's destiny.

title: All the Light We Cannot See
author: Anthony Doerr
summary: A blind French girl and a German boy's paths collide in occupied France during World War II in this Pulitzer Prize-winning novel.

title: Allegiant
author: Veronica Roth
summary: The explosive conclusion to the Divergent trilogy reveals the secrets behind Tris's dystopian society.

title: Angels and Demons
author: Dan Brown
summary: Harvard symbologist Robert Langdon races to stop an ancient secret society after a scientist is murdered and a deadly device is hidden in Vatican City.

title: The Art of Racing in the Rain
author: Garth Stein
summary: A philosophical dog named Enzo narrates the story of his owner, an aspiring race car driver, and the family's ups and downs.

title: Ashen Winter
author: Mike Mullin
summary: Months after a supervolcano eruption devastates the country, Alex and Darla search for Alex's missing parents in a frozen, lawless landscape.

title: Ashfall
author: Mike Mullin
summary: When the Yellowstone supervolcano erupts, a teenager must survive the ash-covered wasteland America has become.

title: An Astronaut's Guide to Life on Earth
author: Chris Hadfield
summary: Astronaut Chris Hadfield shares life lessons learned from decades of training and nearly 4,000 hours in space.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
summary: A practical framework for building good habits and breaking bad ones through small, consistent changes.

title: Better Than the Movies
author: Lynn Painter
summary: A hopeless romantic enlists her charming neighbor's help to win over her longtime crush, with predictably complicated results.

title: Big Mouth and Ugly Girl
author: Joyce Carol Oates
summary: Two unlikely allies band together after a joking comment gets one of them accused of planning violence at school.

title: Blindsided
author: Chelsea Catherine
summary: Two women navigate a Key West mayoral campaign, growing closer even as political pressures threaten to tear them apart.

title: Blink: The Power of Thinking Without Thinking
author: Malcolm Gladwell
summary: An exploration of snap judgments and the science behind split-second decision-making.

title: The Book Thief
author: Markus Zusak
summary: A foster girl in Nazi Germany finds solace in stealing books and shares them with her neighbors and a hidden Jewish refugee.

title: Bossypants
author: Tina Fey
summary: Comedian Tina Fey recounts her path from awkward teen to Saturday Night Live star with characteristic wit.

title: The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas
author: John Boyne
summary: A young German boy befriends a boy on the other side of the fence at a concentration camp, unaware of the horror surrounding them.

title: The Boys in the Boat
author: Daniel James Brown
summary: Nine working-class rowers overcome the odds to compete for Olympic gold at the 1936 Berlin Games.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
summary: Stephen Hawking's landmark exploration of the origins and nature of the universe, written for a general audience.

title: Bringing Down the House
author: Ben Mezrich
summary: A group of MIT students use math skills to count cards and win millions at blackjack in Las Vegas casinos.

title: The Children of Willesden Lane
author: Mona Golabek & Lee Cohen
summary: A young Jewish pianist is sent from Vienna to London on the Kindertransport, holding onto her dream of a musical career.

title: Chinese Handcuffs
author: Chris Crutcher
summary: A teenager trains for an Ironman triathlon to cope with the guilt and grief of his brother's suicide.

title: The Color of Water
author: James McBride
summary: A Black man recounts his childhood and his white mother's remarkable, hidden past.

title: Crazy Rich Asians
author: Kevin Kwan
summary: A woman discovers her boyfriend's family is shockingly wealthy when she travels with him to Singapore for the summer.

title: Crooked Kingdom
author: Leigh Bardugo
summary: Kaz Brekker and his crew attempt a dangerous heist in this sequel to Six of Crows.

title: The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time
author: Mark Haddon
summary: An autistic teenager investigates the death of a neighbor's dog, uncovering family secrets along the way.

title: Daisy Jones & the Six
author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
summary: An oral history-style novel about the rise and dramatic breakup of a 1970s rock band.

title: The Darkest Minds
author: Alexandra Bracken
summary: After a mysterious disease kills most of America's children, those who survive with terrifying new abilities are hunted by the government.

title: The Da Vinci Code
author: Dan Brown
summary: Symbologist Robert Langdon uncovers a centuries-old conspiracy after a curator is murdered in the Louvre.

title: The Death Cure
author: James Dashner
summary: Thomas and the Gladers face their final test against WICKED in the conclusion to the Maze Runner trilogy.

title: The Devil in the White City
author: Erik Larson
summary: The true story of a serial killer operating in the shadow of the 1893 Chicago World's Fair.

title: Divergent
author: Veronica Roth
summary: In a society divided into five factions, Tris must choose where she belongs while hiding a dangerous secret about who she is.

title: A Dog's Purpose
author: W. Bruce Cameron
summary: A dog is reincarnated through several lives, each time discovering more about love, loyalty, and his purpose.

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
summary: On the desert planet Arrakis, young Paul Atreides is drawn into a struggle for control of the universe's most valuable resource.

title: Eleanor and Park
author: Rainbow Rowell
summary: Two misfit teens fall in love over comic books and mixtapes during one unforgettable school year.

title: The Element
author: Sir Ken Robinson
summary: Ken Robinson examines how people find and cultivate their natural talents and passions.

title: Endurance
author: Alfred Lansing
summary: The gripping true account of explorer Ernest Shackleton's fight for survival after his ship became trapped in Antarctic ice.

title: Everything, Everything
author: Nicola Yoon
summary: A girl allergic to the outside world risks everything for a chance at first love with the boy next door.

title: The 5th Wave
author: Rick Yancey
summary: After alien invasions have devastated the planet, Cassie must decide who she can trust to survive the next wave.

title: Five Feet Apart
author: Rachael Lippincott
summary: Two teens with cystic fibrosis fall for each other despite being forbidden from getting closer than six feet apart.

title: Freakonomics
author: Steven D. Levitt & Stephen J. Dubner
summary: An unconventional look at the hidden economics behind everyday questions, from crime to parenting.

title: The Future of Us
author: Jay Asher & Carolyn Mackler
summary: Two 1996 teens accidentally log onto Facebook fifteen years before it exists, glimpsing their future selves.

title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
author: Holly Jackson
summary: A student's school project to solve a years-old murder uncovers secrets that put her own life at risk.

title: The Hate U Give
author: Angie Thomas
summary: After witnessing a police officer fatally shoot her friend, a teenager must find her voice and decide whether to speak out.

title: The Help
author: Kathryn Stockett
summary: Black maids in 1960s Mississippi risk everything to share their stories with a young white writer.

title: The Hot Zone
author: Richard Preston
summary: A terrifying true account of the Ebola virus and the scientists racing to contain it.

title: The Hotel on the Corner of Bitter and Sweet
author: Jamie Ford
summary: A man revisits his childhood friendship with a Japanese girl, separated when her family was sent to an internment camp during WWII.

title: House Rules
author: Jodi Picoult
summary: A teen with Asperger's becomes the prime suspect in a murder investigation due to behaviors misread as guilt.

title: The Hunger Games Complete Trilogy
author: Suzanne Collins
summary: Katniss Everdeen volunteers to fight to the death in a televised competition to save her younger sister.

title: I Am Malala
author: Malala Yousafzai
summary: Malala Yousafzai recounts standing up to the Taliban for her right to an education, and surviving an assassination attempt.

title: I, Robot
author: Isaac Asimov
summary: A classic collection of short stories exploring the ethics and unintended consequences of artificial intelligence.

title: If He Had Been With Me
author: Laura Nowlin
summary: Two childhood best friends drift apart, only to realize too late how much they meant to each other.

title: If I Stay
author: Gayle Forman
summary: After a car accident leaves her in a coma, a teenager must decide whether to fight to live or let go.

title: The Immortal Life of Henrietta Lacks
author: Rebecca Skloot
summary: The true story of a poor Black woman whose cells, taken without her knowledge, became one of medicine's most important tools.

title: The Infinite Sea
author: Rick Yancey
summary: Cassie and her allies fight to survive as the alien invaders close in on humanity's last defenders.

title: An Invisible Thread
author: Laura Schroff
summary: The true story of an unlikely friendship between a busy executive and a young homeless boy that changed both their lives.

title: In Real Life
author: Joey Graceffa
summary: YouTube star Joey Graceffa opens up about his journey from a difficult childhood to online stardom.

title: Into Thin Air
author: Jon Krakauer
summary: A firsthand account of the deadly 1996 Mount Everest disaster.

title: Insurgent
author: Veronica Roth
summary: As factions collapse into chaos, Tris must decide who she can trust while grappling with grief and guilt.

title: Ironman
author: Chris Crutcher
summary: A teen at war with his father and his coaches channels his anger into training for a triathlon.

title: It's Kind of a Funny Story
author: Ned Vizzini
summary: An overwhelmed teenager checks himself into a psychiatric ward and finds unexpected perspective on his life.

title: The Kite Runner
author: Khaled Hosseini
summary: A man returns to Taliban-controlled Afghanistan to atone for betraying his childhood friend decades earlier.

title: The Last Shot: City Streets, Basketball Dreams
author: Darcy Frey
summary: A journalist follows four talented young basketball players from Coney Island chasing a way out of poverty.

title: The Last Star
author: Rick Yancey
summary: The final battle for Earth's survival concludes the 5th Wave trilogy.

title: Life of Pi
author: Yann Martel
summary: A boy survives a shipwreck and months at sea sharing a lifeboat with a Bengal tiger, questioning faith and storytelling along the way.

title: The Little Giant of Aberdeen County
author: Tiffany Baker
summary: An unusually large woman navigates a small town's cruelty and her own family's secrets.

title: Lone Survivor
author: Marcus Luttrell
summary: A Navy SEAL recounts the true story of a mission gone wrong in Afghanistan and his fight to survive.

title: The Magicians
author: Lev Grossman
summary: A disillusioned teen is admitted to a secret college of magic, discovering that magic doesn't solve his problems the way he imagined.

title: The Martian
author: Andy Weir
summary: An astronaut stranded alone on Mars must use ingenuity and science to survive until rescue is possible.

title: Matched: Volume 1
author: Ally Condie
summary: In a society that selects your future spouse, career, and even death, one girl discovers a rebellious spark of choice.

title: The Maze Runner
author: James Dashner
summary: A boy with no memory must navigate a deadly, ever-changing maze alongside other trapped teens.

title: Memoirs of a Teenage Amnesiac
author: Gabrielle Zevin
summary: After a head injury erases four years of memory, a teenager must relearn who she is and who she wants to become.

title: Moneyball
author: Michael Lewis
summary: The story of how a small-budget baseball team used data analysis to compete with the richest teams in the league.

title: The Mortal Instruments Series
author: Cassandra Clare
summary: A girl discovers a hidden world of demon hunters after witnessing a murder no one else can see.

title: Mr. Penumbra's 24-Hour Bookstore
author: Robin Sloan
summary: A young web designer stumbles into a strange bookstore and a centuries-old puzzle blending old books with new technology.

title: My Sister's Keeper
author: Jodi Picoult
summary: A girl conceived to save her sister's life through medical donations finally sues her parents for the right to her own body.

title: The Namesake
author: Jhumpa Lahiri
summary: An Indian-American man grapples with identity and his unusual name across two cultures.

title: Never Let Me Go
author: Kazuo Ishiguro
summary: Students at an idyllic English boarding school slowly uncover the dark truth about their purpose in life.

title: The Night Circus
author: Erin Morgenstern
summary: A magical competition between two young illusionists unfolds within a mysterious circus that only opens at night.

title: The Nightingale
author: Kristin Hannah
summary: Two sisters take vastly different paths of resistance during the Nazi occupation of France.

author: George Orwell
summary: In a totalitarian future where Big Brother watches everyone, a man risks everything for independent thought and forbidden love.

title: On the Devil's Court
author: Carl Deuker
summary: A struggling basketball player believes he may have made a deal with the devil to become a star athlete.

title: The Once and Future King
author: T. H. White
summary: A retelling of the legend of King Arthur, from his tutelage under Merlyn to his rise as king of the Britons.

title: One of Us is Lying
author: Karen M. McManus
summary: Five students walk into detention, but only four walk out alive, and each has a motive worth hiding.

title: Orphan Train
author: Christina Baker Kline
summary: An elderly woman's hidden past as an orphan-train rider intertwines with a troubled teen's search for her own identity.

title: Outliers
author: Malcolm Gladwell
summary: Malcolm Gladwell examines the hidden factors, from culture to timing, behind extraordinary success.

title: The Overachievers: The Secret Lives of Driven Kids
author: Alexandra Robbins
summary: A look at the intense pressure high-achieving students face in today's hyper-competitive college admissions landscape.

title: Painting the Black
author: Carl Deuker
summary: A high school baseball player must choose between loyalty to a teammate and his own sense of integrity.

title: Paper Towns
author: John Green
summary: A boy follows clues left by the girl he's loved from afar after she vanishes in the middle of the night.

title: Paranoid Park
author: Blake Nelson
summary: A teenage skateboarder must decide whether to confess to an accidental killing no one else witnessed.

title: The Paris Wife
author: Paula McLain
summary: A fictionalized account of Ernest Hemingway's first marriage amid the glamour of 1920s Paris.

title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
author: Oscar Wilde
summary: A young man's portrait ages and decays in his place while he pursues a life of unpunished vice.

title: P.S. I Still Love You
author: Jenny Han
summary: Lara Jean navigates new romance and old secrets in this sequel to To All the Boys I've Loved Before.

title: Ranger's Apprentice Series
author: John Flanagan
summary: A boy once feared by his village is chosen to train as a Ranger, learning the secretive skills that protect the kingdom.

title: Ready Player One
author: Ernest Cline
summary: In a bleak future, a teenager searches for a hidden prize inside a vast virtual reality by mastering 1980s pop culture trivia.

title: Red Rising
author: Pierce Brown
summary: A miner on Mars infiltrates the ruling class to bring down the oppressive system that controls his people.

title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
author: Robert Kiyosaki
summary: A personal finance classic contrasting two father figures' very different lessons about money and wealth.

title: The Road
author: Cormac McCarthy
summary: A father and son struggle to survive their trek across a devastated, ash-covered America.

title: Room
author: Emma Donoghue
summary: A woman and her young son, held captive for years in a small room, experience the outside world for the first time.

title: The Rule of Four
author: Ian Caldwell & Dustin Thomason
summary: Two Ivy League students become entangled in a murder connected to a centuries-old coded manuscript.

title: Sarah's Key
author: Tatiana de Rosnay
summary: A journalist uncovers the tragic story of a Jewish girl arrested with her family in 1942 Paris.

title: The Secret Life of Bees
author: Sue Monk Kidd
summary: A girl and her caretaker flee to a town connected to her late mother, finding refuge with a family of beekeeping sisters.

title: The Selection
author: Kiera Cass
summary: Thirty-five girls compete for the heart of a prince and a chance to escape their rigid caste system.

title: The Seven Husbands of Evelyn Hugo
author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
summary: A reclusive Hollywood icon finally reveals the truth about her many marriages, loves, and long-held secrets.

title: The Scorch Trials
author: James Dashner
summary: Thomas and the other Gladers face a new, deadlier challenge across a scorched, devastated landscape.

title: Scythe
author: Neal Shusterman
summary: In a world that has conquered death, two teens apprentice as Scythes, the only people allowed to end human life.

title: Shadow of the Wind
author: Carlos Ruiz Zafón
summary: A boy's discovery of a mysterious book draws him into one of postwar Barcelona's darkest secrets.

title: The Silent Patient
author: Alex Michaelides
summary: A psychotherapist becomes obsessed with treating a woman who shot her husband and then never spoke again.

title: Six of Crows
author: Leigh Bardugo
summary: A criminal prodigy assembles a crew of outcasts to pull off a deadly, high-stakes heist.

title: The Space Between Us
author: Thrity Umrigar
summary: Two women, a wealthy employer and her servant, form an unlikely bond across India's rigid caste divide.

title: Spy High: Mission One
author: A. J. Butcher
summary: Six teenagers train to become secret agents at a school unlike any other.

title: State of Fear
author: Michael Crichton
summary: An environmental lawyer uncovers a conspiracy behind a series of engineered natural disasters.

title: St. Lucy's Home for Girls Raised by Wolves
author: Karen Russell
summary: A collection of eerie, imaginative stories set in the swamps of the Florida Everglades.

title: The Summer I Turned Pretty
author: Jenny Han
summary: A girl's lifelong summers at the beach take a turn when she realizes she has feelings for both of her best friends' sons.

title: Sweetness at the Bottom of the Pie
author: Alan Bradley
summary: An eleven-year-old aspiring chemist investigates a murder in her family's English manor in 1950.

title: The Tattooist of Auschwitz
author: Heather Morris
summary: A Slovakian Jewish prisoner is forced to tattoo his fellow prisoners at Auschwitz, where he falls in love against all odds.

title: To All the Boys I've Loved Before
author: Jenny Han
summary: A girl's secret love letters are accidentally mailed to every boy she's ever loved, turning her life upside down.

title: Turtles All the Way Down
author: John Green
summary: A teenager living with anxiety and OCD gets swept into a mystery involving a fugitive billionaire.

title: Two Can Keep a Secret
author: Karen McManus
summary: A new girl in town uncovers dangerous secrets tied to a homecoming queen's long-ago death.

title: Unbroken
author: Laura Hillenbrand
summary: The true story of Olympic runner Louis Zamperini's survival after his plane crashed in the Pacific during WWII.

title: Understanding Comics
author: Scott McCloud
summary: A comic book about comics, exploring the history, art, and meaning of the medium.

title: A Visit from the Goon Squad
author: Jennifer Egan
summary: An interconnected set of stories follows a record executive and the people whose lives intersect with his over decades.

title: The Wal-Mart Effect
author: Charles Fishman
summary: An examination of how the world's largest retailer has reshaped wages, communities, and the global economy.

title: Watchmen
author: Alan Moore & Dave Gibbons
summary: A group of retired vigilantes investigates a conspiracy to murder superheroes in this landmark graphic novel.

title: Water for Elephants
author: Sara Gruen
summary: A veterinary student joins a Depression-era traveling circus, finding both wonder and danger under the big top.

title: We Were Liars
author: E. Lockhart
summary: A wealthy family's private island holds a devastating secret that a girl must piece back together after losing her memory.

title: What is the What?
author: Dave Eggers
summary: A fictionalized account of a Sudanese refugee's harrowing journey as one of the 'Lost Boys' and his new life in America.

title: Where the Crawdads Sing
author: Delia Owens
summary: A girl raised alone in the marshes of North Carolina becomes the prime suspect when a local man is found dead.

title: Where You Go Is Not Who You'll Be
author: Frank Bruni
summary: A journalist challenges the belief that a student's worth is determined by which colleges accept them.

title: Will Grayson, Will Grayson
author: John Green & David Levithan
summary: Two teenagers who share the same name cross paths one night in Chicago, changing both their lives.

title: Wonder
author: R.J. Palacio
summary: A boy with a facial difference starts mainstream school for the first time, hoping to be seen as more than his appearance.
